Okay, so the first thing I wanna talk about, because this is, I think probably the most important thing to understand about OptinFunnels is your list is really like, that's the most important thing about your entire business.
Someday a lot of you guys, your goal after you start building your business is gonna be able to sell it.
And what's interesting is the number one thing that people look at typically, I mean besides cash flow and things like that, when they're purchasing a business is the customer list.
That's like the most valuable asset you will have inside of your entire business, your subscriber and your customer lists.
And I was lucky enough that when I got started in this business, my very first mentor was a guy named Mark Joyner.
And Mark was like the list building king.
And he pounded it through my head at a very, very young age.
You know, my very first few months online that the money's in the list, you have to build a list, you have to focus on building your list and building your relationship with the list.
And that's how you make money.
And so I started doing that.
And it's funny, because I was doing this process about the same time that if you guys remember, you know, nine or 10 years ago online, all these other things started popping up.
You had AdSense was popping up and people were making insane amounts of money and you had all these other like distractions or they were popping up.
And I remember looking around, I had friends are making $100, $200, $300 a month with AdSense sites.
I remember I wanted to do that, but I was so focused on this list building thing.
And it was a little bit slower at first.
It kind of started organically growing.
I remember being frustrated because all you guys were making all this money and I was focusing on this list thing.
And because that's what my mentor kept telling me, excuse me, and a little while later, and the whole like Google AdSense thing lasted for a year or two.
And then literally overnight it disappeared.
Friends I had that were making half million bucks a month, the money was gone instantly.
And a lot of them went bankrupt because of it.
They lost homes and everything kind of fell apart for them.
And luckily for me is that I had been listening to my mentor and I had focused my entire time on building a list.
And when everything went south for them, I still had this list and my list kept growing and I kept adding people to it and it saved me.
And it saved me probably five or six times in my business where things are scary and we need more income and I can send some emails out to my email list and money comes back in.
And so if I look at what my key performance indicator, my KPI, my number one KPI in my company that we focus on is our list size and how many people are we adding each day, each month, each year to our list because that's really the best gauge of how stable and how good your business is, is your list.
So I wanna stress that that should be your guys the number one goal.
If it's not yet, make that the number one goal, is building out your list.
And so for me, like I said, I built them, I started building my list and that became our whole focus and everything I'm gonna be showing you guys here today in the OptinFunnels module, it's gonna help you to understand how we build our list and it's like college behind it and what we do and what we don't do and all those kind of things and it should be a lot of fun.
So I'm excited.
All right, the other thing I wanna show you guys real quick, open up Paintbrush.
I did this on the webinars as well, so you guys have seen this before.
But I wanna talk really quick about list building math because this is all the stuff that like Mark Joyner used to drill in my head and I'm so grateful that he did.
So I wanna kind of help you guys to see this because it literally has changed my life, understanding it.
So the first thing is list building math, right?
So what we found out was when I first started building my list, I remember the first month I added 217 people to my list, right?
So each of those people was a little person here.
What we found out is that on average, we were making about $1 for each person who's on my list, which means from that, we made, I think that month I made around 200 bucks.
And I was just ecstatic, I was the coolest thing in the world.
Then I kept growing my list and within like a month and a half, I'd got it to about the point where I had about 1,000 people and sure enough, my income that month was about 1,000 bucks.
Which was awesome, so I kept focusing on it, focusing on it and after a couple more months, I got a point where my list was about 10,000 people and sure enough, just like Clockwork, my income that month was about $10,000.
And it's gone up from there to the point where we had 100,000 people on our list and then 200 and 300 and 500,000 people.
And so for me, like I said, this is like my driving metric is how big is my list?
Because the bigger my list is, the more money's gonna come back here.
And so the metric I want you guys thinking about is you start getting this whole spilling mentality is you should be earning about $1 for each person who's on your list per month.
Wish we had a real whiteboard, it was a lot easier to write than chalkboard or than paintbrush, but there you go.
So $1 per month for every single person who's on your email list, okay?
So it's important for you guys from day number one to start thinking about that and start focusing on that.
I remember when I was first getting this whole business thing and trying to learn different ways to make money, there's a lot of people doing a lot of things and I looked at everything, I looked at selling stuff on eBay, I looked at Craigslist, I looked at everything and one of the big things that was big for me and most people was real estate.
And so I actually went out there and in one year I bought six houses, okay?
And this is kind of the model for real estate, right?
For a home, there's my awesome home drawing.
So what happens with a typical home is you go out there and you've got to purchase a home.
Now luckily the bank's helping you, right?
Let's just say I spend $100,000 buying this home, okay?
So there's $100,000 and then after I get it, I gotta fix it up and I gotta pay money to paint the walls and make it really, really nice, right?
So that's kind of this whole process here.
And then after all of a sudden, then my goal is to go get some renters, right?
If I get these renters to come and get in the house, then my goal is to cash flow this property, right?
And so for my house, I remember I was paying like 800 bucks a month and my renters are, 800 bucks a month in my mortgage and my renters were paying me 1200.
I was cash flowing like $400 a month, right?
And that was like pretty dang cool, like sweet.
And so I am getting six houses, you know, so the peak with six houses times 400 bucks, we're making like 24, 100 bucks a month, right?
Now that's all seemed pretty good until after the very first year of having renters in one of my homes, I remember I kept getting letters from the state saying that my renter was doing things to the neighbors and one of my renters like flashed my neighbors and then like peed on the neighbor's lawn and all these things, I got these complaints coming to me because of my renter, which was driving me crazy.
So after a year, we decided to nicely just say, hey, you know, we're not gonna renew your lease, sorry.
And instead of them just nicely leaving, they decided to take like a sledgehammer to our walls and knock out our walls and steal my pipes and leave our house with like 30 or $40,000 with the damage.
So my little $400 a month I'd made over a year, that return on my investment immediately disappeared again and it was horrible, okay?
And so I started hating real estate, but I wanted this cash flow, right?
That's what everyone talks about in business and that's why people invest all this money into real estate.
Okay, and so I looked at how much risk and liability and headaches and everything went into this to get $400 a month.
Now I started looking back at list building, now if I wanna make $400 a month, what do I have to do?
All I have to do is take people through an opt-in funnel, which we're gonna be showing you guys today in excruciating detail, an opt-in funnel.
Typically you're gonna spend a little bit of money to get somebody to join that funnel.
They go through the process and then the end of it, right?
You've got a subscriber.
And so my goal, if I can get 400 people to go through this opt-in funnel and this end up on the side of it, now I've got a list that can and should be cash flowing me about $400 a month, okay?
Now typically, just so you guys know, and it'll be different for every market and every industry and I don't know what business you're in, so I can't tell you for sure, but we might spend anywhere from 50 cents maybe like $2 to get an opt-in, right?
So if I go through this and I need to get 400 opt-ins, I spend $800 one time, okay?
So it'll cost me $800 one time in list-building fees to get a list and now it makes me $400 a month, okay?
Now I don't know about you, but I would so much rather pay $800 one time to get $400 a month than $100,000 plus the rehab, plus the liability, plus the bank loans, plus all this stuff to the same $400 a month, right?
So if we might start looking at that way, I said, you know, I'm gonna start focusing 100% of my time, effort, money, energy, everything on this growing the list because this is the better opportunity.
It is all day long and so that's really one of the things why I wanted it to start with opt-in funneling to you guys because if we can start right, we're gonna end right.
And if I can get your minds focusing on building a list, building a list, building a list, everything we do, every type of funnel we create, everything I'm gonna be showing you guys over the next six weeks or so is all focusing on one core goal and that's to build a list and after you have that list, how to convert them into customers, okay?
And these sales funds we'll be walking through are gonna be teaching you guys all of these different things.
So that's kind of the game plan.
So I wanna make sure you guys had that and you understood it and you got excited by it because if it is, man, this becomes like the most exciting game in the world.
You know, you're putting in a little time or effort or money to build a list and that list has grown.
Then it's how do I communicate?
How do I build a relationship?
How do I connect with these people so they wanna buy for me?
And then it makes creating products fun and all the other cool things we'll be sharing with you guys over the next six weeks.

Okay, so funnel hacks.
All right, so now we'll talk about kind of the history of how building a list has kind of evolved since I got started online.
And also, some of you guys have been around for as long as me, some of you guys are brand new.
This is kind of the process, how it started.
It first started out with these little guys right here.
Can you guys remember these things?
We call them popups.
All right, and so what happened back when I first got started online is that you would go to a website and when you did, all these little popups would pop up saying, do you want my newsletter here?
Congratulations, you won this.
Give me your email address and they would pop up like crazy, right?
And I'm sure you guys remember this as well.
And it drove people crazy.
Okay, and I remember for the first two or three years, people like internet sucks because I go on the internet and all these popups are coming up and I can't even get anywhere and they crash my browser and it's this horrible thing, right?
And so people complained about it.
But what was happening is these marketers, really smart marketers like you and like me, they understood that these popups would pop up and it would help somebody to join your list.
Also you're building a list.
And I know people that build email lists of millions of people, 100% through these popups.
Okay, and then they were making insane amounts of money with it.
The problem though is that the user experience was so bad that everyone's complaining and soon all of the browsers that came out, like this is all pre-chrome, but your internet explorer and Firefox and things like that all started building popup blockers and that basically blocked all these popups.
We're now, you couldn't have a popup on your website.
And so marketers who are used to building these big used lists, all of a sudden they went from adding a couple thousand people a day to their list to adding almost nobody and they started getting frustrated in.
And luckily there's some really smart marketing minds out there who are trying to figure out an answer and how do we change this process and how do we make this something that's gonna work long term.
And so let me pull this up again.
So what they did is, and I'm not sure, I've heard three or four people claim they invented it.
I don't know who invented it.
But you can hear the old concept, right?
You go to website, website would show up and all of a sudden something would pop up, boom, for your email address, right?
And that was how this whole thing worked.
And there were some smart guys who said, what if we change this process?
What if we took this little popup right here instead of having a popup on my site?
What if I moved it over here and I made that the first thing that somebody actually saw?
And they give me the email address and if they give me their email address then they get to see my website over here.
So just kind of taking these pages and moving the order around.
And I remember when they were first doing this everyone was talking about like, hey so-and-so is putting up this thing and they call them squeeze pages and gateway pages and landing pages and lead pages and opt-in pages and there's a million different names for it.
The concepts always the same.
Was kind of moving this process around.
And people were like thinking like, I remember people saying, like big discussions on the message boards back then about how stupid they were.
Like they're so stupid now, instead of having 100% of people hit their website, 100% will hit this page, then only like 20 or 30 in the email address means only 20% of the people ever actually see your sales page and they're gonna make one 20th as much money and that was this big concern, right?
And luckily these guys didn't listen.
They went and tested this stuff like crazy and eventually found out that it worked.
They found out that what was interesting is that the people who did opt-in were the same people who were gonna buy anyway.
And so they may have a smaller percent here but the sales stayed the same, okay?
So someone's gonna buy no matter what, they're gonna give you email and show up.
So less people show up on this page but everyone who does show up are the people who would have bought anyway, okay?
But the second thing what made this so powerful is now all these people are being added to an email list and they're gonna be added to an email list and then they create a follow-up sequence where they can go back and they can repromote the page and then they can send another email and they can send multiple emails back to the software to sell something and then somewhere down the line they can start selling different products, right?
And it completely transformed the whole business model and transformed how all of us guys were making money online.
And it gave us the ability to start growing a list really rapidly again to be able to follow up by our customers.
I remember reading at a time and I don't know and I don't think the stats are the same but back then they said that the average person had to see your sales message seven times before they'd be comfortable enough to make a buying decision with you.
And again, I don't think that's still true today but the ability to follow up to something now all of a sudden made it so you could close more people because you can obviously follow up.
People don't always make impulse decisions and so the other way to follow up then it's gonna increase your conversions, okay?
So that's kind of what happened here and that was the rage everyone was talking about.
Squeeze Pages was the biggest thing in the world when that first came out and I remember I got excited and we started jumping in and doing like crazy, okay?
I still remember the very first Squeeze Page I ever saw was Eben Pagans and this is what it looked like right here and this was a site called werdating.com and werdating.com he built through this Squeeze Page and look how ugly it is you guys.
It's very basic, very easy and I wanna kind of walk you through this process so you can understand because you understand the power behind it.
I mean, he built, Eben built a company who's doing like $20 million a year off of this Squeeze Page.
They drive all the traffic to here and if you look at it, it's very simple.
It's got a headline, you're about to learn secrets that most men will never know about women and inside you're gonna learn the kiss test, how to tell if she's ready to be kissed.
Number two, the difference between how men and women think about dating and why most women want to keep you from being successful.
Number three, how to use secret body language to keep women's attention.
Number four, how to approach new women that you'd like to meet and exactly what to do or what to say to start a conversation without any pickup lines, fun places to meet women that are free, on and on and on, right?
And then just use your first name and ballot email address as your password and then click on the free instant access button to enter.
Use the same password when returning, all information's kept 100% confidential, blah, blah, blah.
So that was the Squeeze Page, right?
I remember seeing this and I was like, this is like the weirdest thing in the world.
There's no pitchers, there's no nothing, but this guy built a $20 million business office and I wanted to figure out what he was doing and why he was doing it.
And so I remember I went through this process and step one is I came and I put my email address in and I clicked on get free instant access and then the next page, and I wish the page was still live, I was grateful I was able to find an image of it online but I can't find the actual pages, they're no longer live.
But on the next page after you opted in, it gave you this first secret here, the kiss test.
Okay, so thanks so much for joining or for subscribing.
Now before we go on, I wanna share with you the kiss test.
I was like, okay, cool.
And what was interesting is that the only thing he shared, literally the kiss test was like one paragraph long and it said, this is the kiss test.
Next time you're with a girl and you've gone on a great date, you walk up to her doorstep, all you wanna do is you go and you lift your hand up and you go reach to her face and you push her hair behind her ear.
Okay, which is not that scary of a thing, you kinda do that, you reach up, put her hair behind your ear and it says, if she wants you to kiss her, then she'll turn her head towards you and if she doesn't, then she'll keep her head where it is.
So if she moves her head towards you, that's the kiss test and that means that she's ready to be kissed and you can move it and kiss her.
She doesn't, then back out and don't risk, you know, like try to kiss her when she rejects you.
And it was just again, one paragraph where he explained that and he said, click here for more information, click there and boom, took him to the actual sales page now where he sold his product.
I remember thinking like, man, how simple is that?
Okay, what he was giving them for their email address, wasn't this huge, elaborate thing, wasn't like, I'm gonna give you a 30 page report and 15 videos and I see people do that all the time nowadays.
It's like, let me give you a million things when you opt in, that's not what it is.
It's giving them one thing that they can't figure out without your answer.
Okay, for this it was the kiss test, it was one paragraph.
Okay, so start thinking about that as we start talking about like the opt-in bait and things we can do and we're gonna go over that in more detail, but think about that.
Like what's your kiss test?
Do you have something a little like that that you can share with somebody that doesn't take that much, but then they have that, they have this little a-ha, we're like, wow, that is really cool.
Okay, the person going through this funnel, you might think, wow, that's something I can actually do next time I'm out with the girl.
You know, I'm always scared, I don't know what to do, I always get awkward.
Now if I just try this, it's non-threatening and I can actually do it, right?
What do you have in your market?
Like what kind of kiss test do you guys have that you can model that with, okay?
All right, so we're gonna dig deeper.
I just wanna kinda show you that as well.
Okay, all right, cool.
Okay, someone's asking, are we gonna learn how to monetize the backend?
Yeah, we're, just hang on, we got six weeks of this, you guys, we're gonna be covering everything.
Those folks in option funnels right now will dig deeper into all the other funnel types and everything else that we do for sure.
All right, so this was the first squeeze page I ever saw, now I told you there's a lot during the funnel hacks training, right?
That you guys signed up for this training.
My whole goal is, is I look out there, I go to everyone's websites and I buy everything, I go through the processes and I'm trying to model, see what they're doing and trying to figure out what's working and then I go back and I test it on my own.
And so I'm gonna show you guys a couple of the pages that I found that really had a big impact on me and it'll help you kind of see where we're gonna be going here in the future of this training.
But the first one, so I looked at Evan's, obviously, and my first three or four squeeze pages I had for years looked almost identical to this.
Obviously I was in a different market, but I modeled the look and the fill, I had a headline, I had inside you're gonna learn, I had five or six bullet points, I modeled it to a T.
If you saw his, you saw mine, you'd think that Evan was selling all sorts of different things because I just, I looked at what worked and I modeled it.
Now, a little while later, I saw this, Ryan Deis told me about this website they had, which was a Forex product.
And I don't think the squeeze page is live anymore because the button's dead.
But I remember looking at this and he told me this was his highest converting squeeze page at the time.
And all it was, was a headline in an opt-in box.
I don't think, wow, that's the weirdest thing in the world.
And so I looked at it and said, free the forgotten trading system that is right in 89.1% of the time, plus how to predict breakouts in the currency market days before they actually occur.
And your email address is below and I'll give you free and access to in the name of the report.
Okay, very simple, very easy.
And it's interesting, if you look at most of the squeeze pages I create nowadays, I still base off of this concept.
All I really want is one headline in an opt-in box.
Okay, I'd say the majority of people who go, and I'm gonna talk about the strategy behind this, but the majority of people who have these landing pages with pictures and images and videos and all this stuff to sell in an opt-in, the most part that's way overdoing it.
It's not necessary, it's not needed.
The core thing you want for building a list, I learned this from Dagan Smith, is a curiosity-based headline.
That'll increase your conversions more than anything else in the world.
Okay, curiosity-based headlines.
Yeah, think about that.
What kind of, what do you have this can cause?
So much curiosity, it's something like, they have to know and they have to put their email address in.
Okay, literally this page is simple guys, right?
I can build some click funnels in about three seconds.
Okay, it's so easy, but this is the key.
The key is the curiosity-based headline.
And I wanna stress that because no matter what template, no matter how you build your funnel, that's the number one secret you guys are getting an amazing opt-in rate.
Okay, it's your headline.
Okay, and it's not so much that it's offering a big promise a lot of times, it just doesn't make sense.
You can't figure it out unless they actually, unless they actually give you your email address.
Okay, so that's what I'm gonna show you that one because this is key in my whole thought process and what we've been doing for the last 10 years.
And I wanna show you guys that here in a little bit.
All right, so that's, okay, so we're talking about pop-ups versus squeeze pages.
And again, this is a very basic squeeze page as well.
Next thing I wanna show you guys is lead madness or ethical bribes.
Now I was trying to find one that's kind of hot and recent right now, and I'm sure you guys have probably seen this one on Facebook.
This is one that, again, digital marketer, Ryan Dyson and Perry Belcher put out.
That's kind of cool.
This one is a lead magnet, okay?
And I think Ryan's the one that coined the name lead magnet.
And basically it's like an ethical bribe, right?
Something you're gonna give away in exchange for the email address.
So not so much curiosity-based, but more like, hey, I've got this really cool thing and if you want it, give me your email address.
So this one's kind of cool.
It's called the Ultimate Social Media Swipe File.
It's got a headline, a couple bullet points.
It's got a really cool image of what the swipe file might look like and then download now button.
And it's really, really cool, right?
An ethical bribe.
So a lot of times I'm making opt-in funnels.
I'm looking for an ethical bribe.
Okay, for instance, swipe file.
We've done them in the past where it's like just a PDF handout of our systems or it's an infographic or maybe it's a 10-step process or maybe it's a really cool article or a video or something, okay?
A lot of times you guys are trying to build a list.
You just gotta find something cool.

And I'll show you as more examples of that here in a minute.
But I'm trying to show you the different types of squeeze pages.
This is like the lead magnet ethical bribe.
It's creating some kind of bribe, some kind of magnet where they want this thing, they've gotta give you their email address, okay?
So here we have pure raw curiosity.
This is where bribing them and exchanging them for something.
So you give me this, I'll give you that.
And the next kind, and this is called the reverse squeeze page.
And this is the one I see a lot of people doing recently and most people don't understand this.
And it's a powerful concept.
It doesn't work on all types of media, but on certain ones it works really, really good.
And reverse squeeze page is basically something like this where you have a training video ahead of time.
So this is like a, I don't know, five or six minute training video that someone comes to you first.
So I'm giving them a whole bunch of content at front first and then I ask them for their email address, okay?
So back here, I'm saying, here's some curiosity, give me your email address and I'll tell you what the answer is.
Here's what I got, it's a really cool thing I'm bribing you with, here you go.
Here, the reverse squeeze is I'm gonna give you so much value that you feel obligated to give me your email address to go get more value on top of it.
Okay, you guys see that?
So those are the three core types of squeeze pages that we use in our company every single day, okay?
Now with, and let me kind of break down.
So if I'm, and we're gonna talk more about traffic throughout this training course, plus you guys have instant access to the whole instant traffic acts, but if I'm doing like, excuse me, email, so if I'm buying solo ads or email ads, I'm typically trying to send them to something that's pure curiosity based like this, right?
Something that they can't figure out.
For email, this is like, this kind of website's one I'm always using.

We also use those as well.
But something that that works, it's 99.9% curiosity driven is what we're focusing our email traffic from, okay?
Now this kind of thing, socially, like the ethical bribe, this works really good for social media.

Now we're gonna transition over to auto responders, okay?
Because auto responders are really the magic behind this.
Okay, now when I first got started online, I didn't have an email auto responder, I didn't know what one was.
And so I used to like, I'd open up, I was using Outlook at the time.
I had this little list of people I was growing, I'd open up Outlook and I would blind carbon copy everyone and I would email out to this in my list of that I was building, right?
I didn't know that there were such things in auto responders, so I would literally go out there and I would start, you know, trying to gather leads.
I'd go to people's websites, I'd see the email, I'd just like grab it and I put it in my little like Excel sheet and I kept building up this list of people the wrong way.
And then I'd go into Outlook and I'd go in and blind carbon copy everyone, send it out and obviously did not work and I got in trouble and I got lots of complaints and it was the wrong way.
The right way is using an auto responder, which an auto responder is a company that basically runs your email newsletters.
So when somebody comes this webpage and gives me their email address, they're instantly added to my auto responder account, okay?
They click on this and they put their email address in, they're automatically added to my email auto responder.
Then my auto responder, I can do two really cool things.
One is I can set up a broadcast.
So let me open a paintbrush again.
All right, so somebody comes through again.
All right, so they come through, make that a little bigger.
So they come to my squeeze page, they join my list, right?
And then I have an email, I can set up an auto responder where day number one, send them this email.
The day number two, send them this email.
The day number three, send them this email.
So I'm sending an auto responder sequence, okay?
I set that up ahead of time.
I can set up for as long as I want, okay?
I typically set up my auto responder sequences and we'll talk about this here in a minute.
I typically set them up for like five or six days.
I've got friends though who are so obsessed with this, they literally have sequences built out for like two or three years in advance.
And that's kind of how they do it.
But for me, I just do five, four, about five days typically.
And I call this my soap opera sequence.
And again, we'll talk about that here in a little bit.
So that's one way.
And then for me, then I transition after that and I start sending a different type of email.
And these are broadcasts.
We're basically, I wake up in the morning, I get an idea for an email, I go and I write it and it clicks in.
So these back here are called broadcasts.
If I can spell that right.
Broadcasts or else call them sign filled.
We'll talk about why here in a minute.
But so soap opera or call them auto responders.

All right, so that's kind of what you can do is I can set up auto responder sequences.

Auto responder sequences.
I can set up broadcasts.
So broadcasts are one offs.
I wake up in the morning, I send an email, boom, it goes out to everybody on my list.

Now, ClickFunnels sends out these emails, but we don't send them from our server.

So SMTP is just, it's kind of like a third-party email sending service.
Okay, the two that we use are sendgrid.com and also mandrill.com.
You can use either of them.
They both work very, very similar.
They're just run by different companies.
MailChimp owns mandrill.com, sendgrid is on my sendgrid.
So they're just two different SMTP mailers.
Now, if you do that, there's a signup process on their side and it's not super easy.
It takes a little bit of time to set up some stuff and domain keys and a bunch of annoying things, but they'll walk you through that process.
Then when you're done, you come back here to my account, click on Settings.
You notice here there's a section that says SMTP email settings.
You click on that and then you can go in there and actually plug in your information.

And if you watch that, this is gonna take you guys through the process of how we communicate with our audience.
The step number one you have to understand is the attractive character.
This is your personality and how you're communicating with your audience.
This has everything to do with how successful you will be with your list.
I get to them people go through and they start building these huge lists and don't make hardly any money.
And it's because they don't understand how to communicate with their audience.
And so come in here and watch, in fact in here there's a bunch of videos.
There's four videos in here that you need to watch that'll help you understand the attractive character and how to design and architect your personal attractive character.
Okay, again, if you want people to open your emails every single day and to buy what you're recommending, you can't be boring, okay?
And I don't know why, but for some reason people start building a list, they're just super boring.
Well, they try to sell stuff all day long.
And if you don't have a relationship with your audience, it won't work.
If you do have a relationship with the audience, they will buy from you over and over and over and over again.
People keep asking Russell, how in the world are you guys selling so much for your high ticket, your 10 year, $25,000 coaching program?
And I said, because I've got an amazing relation to my list, they know my voice, they know my stories, they, we communicate that way.
Okay, so everything inside here and the attractive character is gonna help you to understand how to craft that personality that's gonna be communicating with your audience and how to do it the right way.
After you have that done, next thing you do is we start focusing on our initial email auto responder sequence.
And we call this a soap opera sequence.
The reason is I learned this initially from a guy named Andre Chaperone, and I'm sure most of you guys know him, I've heard of him.
He created an amazing course called auto responder madness, where he teaches you how to build these soap opera sequences.
Okay, and it's a soap opera because it's typical, it's just like a regular soap opera where each episode like draws you into the next episode and then that episode draws you into the next, right?
And eventually you have to go watch, you know, 16 years of soap operas because the story never actually ends, right?
And so Andre in his course, he teaches how to build these huge intense soap opera sequences.
And I tried that before, but I've never been able to do it.
But instead what I do is I create very short, like I said, about five day soap opera sequences with the entire goal is when a new customer, so some dude over here joins my list, and they come in here, then my number one goal is not to sell them, my number one goal is to build the relationship with the attractive character, and I do that 100% through this initial soap opera sequence, okay?
And then from there I transitioned to sign full talk about, my first goal is building that soap opera.
So if you come here with soap opera training in here, there's a whole video of here, it kind of goes through how I do it, why I do it.
I show you guys how I write them out and everything.
And so watch this video, show you how we write them out, how we kind of structure it.
Again, if you come back to the homepage inside of the funnel course, you guys, I also gave you some outlines and stuff like that.
In fact, I need to add those here in the members area.
Oh, here's the, actually there it is, I was wrong.
Cool, so this is how we structure our soap opera sequence, where day number one is all about how we set the stage, day number two is our high drama, our backstory, our wall, day three is epiphany, one thing, day four hidden benefits, and day five is urgency.
So I'll be putting in more training in the members area about all these different emails in the sequence, but that's how I do my mini soap operas in my first five days, okay?
Now after somebody's gone through my soap opera sequence, then I transition over to what we call our sign filled emails, which a sign filled email is basically kind of like the show sign filled, okay?
Sign filled is a show about nothing, right?
And so day sign filled emails are emails kind of about nothing.
Where every day I wake up and I send an email about just what's happening in my life, what's cool, and what's going on, and these emails honestly are like 90% entertainment, 10% content, actually 90% entertainment, 10% content, 1% pitch, okay?
You have to have the pitch in there, otherwise it doesn't really work.
So who here wants to see one of my sign filled emails?
People never understand how well these things work, but they work, so let me pull up one real quick.
So this is a sign filled email, I'm gonna read, excuse me.
Okay, I'm gonna read this.
So both of these emails, let me read two of these to you guys.
Well at least one, I'll read one for sure.
But this email made over $100,000 when I sent it out to my list that had been warming up with my attractive character.
So this is the email.
So subject line was true story, he flushed $20 million down the toilet today.
So yesterday we had this guy apply for my Inner Circle program.
I saw his app come through and I actually was really excited because he's in the golf market.
Now I'm no golfer, but I've got a lot of friends who had $20 million plus in the golf market online.
I saw his product and I knew it was a home run.
So the coach who was gonna call him back asked me for my opinion on his business before he called him.
And I sat down for 10 minutes and pulled up, first is three major competitors, second I showed her every site they were successfully buying traffic from, third I showed her the top three converting ads for each of his competitors, fourth I showed her the sales funds that were converting and the main reason why he was not.
I then showed her the two media buyers I would use the fives in the golf market, both of you, both who can send over a thousand cells a day consistently and armed with this information she called the guy up.
Now he was a little cocky and rightfully so.
He sold over 100,000 units of his product on TV.
Yet for some reason he couldn't figure out this pesky internet thing.
She started sharing with him some of my ideas and then he stopped her.
Look I've read 20 books on internet marketing there isn't a single thing that Russell could teach me that I don't already know.
So she tried to explain look you could read a million books on Jiu Jitsu that's not gonna really help you in a street fight.
I thought that was pretty funny but what happened next was just sad.
He said well Russell doesn't know anything about golf and then he hung up.
Now while he was right about me not knowing anything about golf I know everything about selling golf stuff online.
I've been doing this now for over 10 years and I personally trained over 2,500 companies here in my office in Boise to do in those 10 years.
I've worked a lot of golf guys and even one golf gal.
I've worked with people in just about every market I can think of except for bowling.
I've never had someone teach bowling come to me which makes me sad because bowling is my third favorite sport behind number one wrestling and number two Jiu Jitsu.
Anyway for about everything else I can think of I've helped map out a funnel, showed them what they were doing wrong, introduced them to my media buyers and showed them what sites to buy ads on and when they should be spending to acquire customers in their specific market.
I then usually intro them to the gurus I know in those areas and after speaking on Dan Kennedy's stages for six years I gotta meet most of the gurus in most industries and it makes it very easy to find connections for those people.
Those are things you can't learn in a book.
Those are things we bring to the table for our inner circle people.
My goal for them is not to teach them more stuff it's to make them more money.

So I wanna talk to you about this concept.
And we have a whole training course called List Hacking that kind of teaches this concept.
But it's really important to understand how this whole industry got started because it'll help you understand how you can speed up this process for yourself.
So if you look back, when I first got started, when I was like 13 years old, I started, I was watching Late Night Info Mercial one night with my dad and this guy named Don the Pre came on and he started talking about placing tiny little classified ads and I got so excited.
I bought his course.
I saved him money and it's a 13 year old bought his make money course.
And then I went out there and I started just becoming obsessed with this stuff.
And I remember I went to the grocery store and they had this magazine, which you guys probably seen it before.
It's called Small Bizot Magazine.
You guys seen that before?
Bizot Magazine.
It's usually like this cartoon people up here on the front of it.
And anyway, that's kind of what it looks like.
So you open it and inside of it, literally, the last time I did this, there were like 140 pages of ads and two articles in this magazine.
It's like 100% Bizot ads, right?
And I remember looking at all these ads, I was like, oh, these are really, really cool.
And so, and each of them, like, if you looked at the ad, there'd be like this page right here, right?
It'd have this ad right here and the ad right here.
And usually on this corner here, they'd have like a phone number for you to call.
So I would go and I'd call the 1-800 number and they would say, call this number for free reports.
I'd call the number and someone over here would pick up the phone and I'd give them my email address and then they would ship me out this letter in the mail for my free report, right?
Free report came to 13-year-old Russell over here.
I got it in the mail, I'm super excited.
I open up this report, I open up and it says long letter that's a report telling about this really cool concept.
And usually the report's like 10 or 12 or I had something like 80 pages long.
I read every one of them, I was so excited.
And the end of it, usually there was something at the end where they're asking for some money, right?
They'd say, if you want more information, then give us, you know, rise to check for 30 bucks and we'll send you something, right?
So then young Russell would go beg his parents for some money.
They'd give you some chores.
I would then go send in my hard earned cash for the kit.
They would then ship me back out this kit.
They would have some stuff in there and then guess what was inside the kit?
They'd have another long form sales letter sell me like, instead of, this little kit was like 37 bucks, then they'd sell me like a $2,000 kit when my, in the kit that came, okay?
That was how this whole thing worked offline.
I thought, man, we're really, really smart, right?
These guys are really smart.
I look at what we do online right now and guys, guess what?
It's pretty similar, okay?
So let me look, this is again, that's old school.
This is what it looks like today.
Today you go into like whatever your favorite website is, a blog or maybe it's Facebook or maybe your email, whatever it is, and you see an ad, right?
So you click on that ad, boom, and it takes you over to this website.
This website is like, hey, give me your email address.
So instead of, instead of give me your phone number here, it just says give me your email address.
And if you give me your email address, I'm gonna send you a free report or a free video, right?
Just give me email address and boom, takes you over here now to a page.
Then if it's a free report, it's a long form sales letter, right?
Or if it's a free video, then what is it?
It is a sales video.
So here's a sales video.
Okay, and then if I buy that sales video, then what do they do?
Then they upsell me on the next thing.
So from here I might buy a $37 product and then maybe the next page, upsell page, they now sell me a $2,000 product, right?
Okay, so it's the exact same process you guys, it's very, very similar.
But instead of going through the whole offline thing, it happens online.
The ad pushes me to a squeeze page to get an email address.
The next page pitches me video and if there are my upsells, okay?
Now the key thing I wanna stress here because this is like my big a-ha that I had because a lot of people struggle like I'm building out this landing page, like what am I, I don't have anything to give people.
I don't have an ethical bribe.
I don't have a cool thing.
I don't wanna make a, I don't wanna report.
I don't wanna make a whole video.
Like what's the, what's, like what am I gonna give them for free?
And what I understood back here what I learned from these old school direct mail guys is I was calling this 1-800 number for free report, right?
When this report showed up in the mail, what was it?
It was a sales letter, okay?
If I'm offering someone a squeeze page, my free report, on the next page, I don't have to go write a new free report.
My sales letter is the free report.
Does that make sense?
Okay, if I'm offering a free video, the video is my sales video.
That's the free video I'm giving away.
Does that make sense, you guys?
Okay, a lot of people get confused they think they have to create a free video and then from there they just push them to another video.
Like, no, the thing that's happening on the sales page, that's the free thing you're offering on your squeeze page, okay?
I wanna make sure that makes sense to you guys because what's gonna happen is, again, this training, we're not talking about your own product.
Our next training, we're gonna be talking about how to build your own products and your upsells and we'll talk about this when we talk about sales funnels.
But for today, we're not talking about that, okay?
I just wanna focus on opt-ins, but if I just have an opt-in, someone joins my list, I've gotta monetize them somehow.
And if I don't have a product yet, how do I do that?
Okay, so that's kind of the question.
So let me show you what I do because it's really simple when you start understanding this, okay?
Again, the key from this graphic to understand is that the free thing that I'm giving away here is the sales thing on the next page.
